high_level_description: A vertical dark-blue science diagram poster showing white sunlight splitting into wavelengths, with short blue waves scattering in all directions off air molecules while a long red wave passes straight through, headlined SHORT WAVES SCATTER MOST, with clear space in the bottom fifth of the frame.

style_description.aesthetics: clean, diagrammatic, modern science explainer, elegant
style_description.lighting: flat even lighting with a soft blue glow around the scattering molecules
style_description.medium: flat vector graphic design, clean geometric
style_description.art_style: minimalist physics infographic poster, generous negative space

compositional_deconstruction.background: Deep navy-blue night-sky gradient background, slightly lighter toward the center, with faint tiny dots suggesting air molecules.

elements.desc: Bold condensed sans-serif headline across two lines, all caps, white, centered at the top.
A wide beam of white sunlight entering from the upper left, drawn as a soft glowing white ray that splits into a small spectrum fan.
A cluster of small glowing spheres representing air molecules, with short tightly-rippled blue waves bouncing off them in many directions, drawn as zigzag blue arrows radiating outward.
A single long lazily-curved red wave passing straight through from left to right, unbothered, drawn as a smooth long-wavelength sine line with an arrowhead.
